steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: When you see your teacher in the morning, what do you say?
questions.hint: We say this at the start of the day when the sun comes up!

questions.question_text: Your friend waves at you and smiles. What can you say?
questions.hint: It's a short, happy word we say when we meet friends!

questions.question_text: What do we say when we want to know someone's name?
questions.hint: We ask this question to learn what to call our new friend!

items.definition: A friendly greeting when you meet someone
A short way to say hello to a friend
What you say when someone asks you a question
Use your ears to hear something carefully

steps.question_text: Match the emoji to the word: 👋

steps.title: First Day Greeting
steps.text: He says, 'Hello!' She says, 'Hi!' They listen and answer the teacher.

items.explanation: Use 'She' as the subject pronoun, not 'Her'
Use 'books' for more than one book
This is correct! We use 'is' with 'who' questions.

steps.audio_text: Good morning, everyone! When you come to class, say 'Hello!' to your friends. Listen carefully when I call your name. Then answer 'Here!' Nice and loud, please!

questions.question: What should you say when you come to class?
What should you do when the teacher calls your name?
What should you say after you listen to your name?

steps.prompt: Let's practice saying hello and asking questions! Listen and repeat after me.

prompts.prompt: Say: Hello! My name is Emma.
Say: I listen to my friend and answer.
prompts.expected_text: I listen to my friend and answer.

questions.question_text: What do you say when you meet someone for the first time?
Who ___ she?
Which word means to use your ears carefully?
True or False: 'Hi' is a short way to say hello.
When the teacher asks you a question, you should ___.

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What do you say when you meet a new friend?
questions.hint: We use this word when we meet someone.

questions.question_text: Who can be your friend?
questions.hint: Think about the people you play with.

questions.question_text: What do friends like to do together?

items.definition: Used to refer to two or more people
Not old or not known before
Having existed for a long time

steps.question_text: Match the emoji to the word: 👥

steps.title: Ben and Kim
steps.text: Today, Ben and Kim join our class. They are new. Ben is happy. Kim is shy.

steps.explanation: Use he or she for one person; they for two.

items.explanation: Use 'He' as the subject pronoun, not 'Him'

items.sentence: They is called Ben and Kim.
I have two friend at school.
He have a new name tag.
My friend is in the classroom.

items.question: ___ is my friend Ben.
items.explanation: Use 'He' as the subject of the sentence

steps.audio_text: Hello, children! Today we have two new friends in our class. They are called Ben and Kim. Ben is seven years old. Kim is six years old. Let's say hello to them!

questions.question: Are Ben and Kim new or old students?

steps.prompt: Practice introducing yourself and talking about friends!

prompts.prompt: Say: He is my new friend.
Say: They are called Ben and Kim.
prompts.expected_text: They are called Ben and Kim.

questions.question_text: What does 'new' mean?
He ___ called Ben.
Which word means 'two or more people'?
Read: 'They are new friends.' Is this sentence correct?
What is your ___ ?

steps.instruction: Let's warm up! Answer these questions about letters!

questions.question_text: Can you find the letter A?
questions.hint: It looks like a triangle on top!

questions.question_text: Which letter comes after A?
questions.hint: A, B, C... What's next after A?

questions.question_text: How many letters are in the alphabet?
questions.hint: It's more than 20 but less than 30!

steps.question_text: Match the emoji to the word: 🔤

steps.title: The Spelling Game
steps.text: In class, they play a spelling game. She spells 'dog'. He spells 'cat'. They clap for the winner!

steps.rule_pattern: ___ is playing the game. Who is ___?

items.explanation: After 'can' use the base verb form

items.sentence: The book is to the table.
items.explanation: Use 'on' to show something is on top of a surface

items.question: He ___ writing words now.
items.explanation: Use 'is' with he for present continuous

steps.audio_text: Hello children! Today we're learning the alphabet. Emma is spelling her name. She says E-M-M-A. That's four letters! Now Tom is spelling his word. He spells C-A-T. Great job, everyone!

questions.question: How many letters are in Emma's name?

steps.prompt: Let's practice speaking! Listen and repeat these sentences.

prompts.prompt: Say: He is playing the game.
Say: Who is spelling the word?

questions.question_text: Which word means the letters A to Z?
She ___ playing the game.
___ is spelling the word?
When you say the letters in 'cat' (C-A-T), you ___ the word.
True or False: 'They is playing' is correct.

steps.instruction: Let's warm up! Answer these questions about saying hello!

questions.question_text: What do you say when you meet someone for the first time?
questions.hint: We use this word to greet people and be friendly.

questions.question_text: When someone asks 'What's your name?', what do you say?
questions.hint: You tell them what people call you.

questions.question_text: How do you ask someone their name?
questions.hint: This question helps you learn what to call your new friend.

steps.title: New Friends
steps.text: They are new. Hello! My name is Tim. Who is she? She is Ana. Let's listen and answer.

steps.audio_text: Hello everyone! My name is Miss Sarah. Today we have a new friend in our class. His name is Max. Let's all say hello to Max!

questions.question: Is Max new or old in the class?

steps.prompt: Let's practice introducing ourselves! Say these sentences out loud.

prompts.prompt: Say: Hello! My name is [your name].
Say: I am [your age] years old.
Now say both sentences together: Hello! My name is [your name]. I am [your age] years old.
prompts.expected_text: Hello! My name is [student's name]. I am [student's age] years old.

questions.question_text: Complete the sentence: ___ is my teacher.
Who is new in the class?
What do we say when we meet someone?
True or False: We use the alphabet to spell our names.
Complete: My friends are here. ___ are happy.
